revision.comment: Text replacement - "四至神}}" to "四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami}}"
revision.text:
{{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Yuki-no-Mikura |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yuki no Mikura 2.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ujikan-chō|ja|宇治館町}} 1, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, within the precincts of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 27 |latitude_sec = 19.5 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 43 |longitude_sec = 26.6 |map= Japan Mie |enshrined_deity= Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami |status= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine |established= Before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 23 (804) |main_hall_style= [[Yuiitsu Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_names= |sacred_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }}
'''Yuki-no-Mikura''' (由貴御倉) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Kōtai Jingū). It is also referred to as '''Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami''' (由貴御倉神).<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref> It is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, standing north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿}}, alongside {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神}}.<ref name="ic"/>
== Overview == Yuki-no-Mikura is located in Ujikan-chō, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, within the precincts of the Inner Shrine. It is ranked 7th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine. The term "Yuki" (由貴) in its name means "pure and free from defilement."<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>
Yuki-no-Mikura was originally a storehouse for offering sacred provisions ({{ill|minie|ja|御贄}}) used in the {{ill|Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja|由貴大御饌}} ceremony.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The former shrine building served as a temporary main hall for {{ill|Yodo Shrine|ja|與杼神社}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, after the shrine was destroyed by fire, from 1975 (Shōwa 50) to 1979 (Shōwa 54).<ref>Yodo Shrine, "[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/syaden.html About the Shrine - Yodo Shrine, Guardian of Fushimi, Kyoto]" (Accessed October 13, 2013)</ref>
The shrine building is surrounded by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence). There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱}} (offering box).
The enshrined deity is Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami (由貴御倉神), who serves as the guardian deity of Yuki-no-Mikura.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus from the south exit of {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, or about 5 minutes south via {{ill|National Route 23|ja|国道23号}} from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami}}.<ref name="ic"/>
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Storehouses in Shinto shrines]] [[ja:由貴御倉]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4
== See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0115.html 由貴御倉（ゆきのみくら）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会

revision.text:
{{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Yuki-no-Mikura |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yuki no Mikura 2.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ujikan-chō|ja|宇治館町}} 1, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, within the precincts of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 27 |latitude_sec = 19.5 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 43 |longitude_sec = 26.6 |map= Japan Mie |enshrined_deity= Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami |status= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine |established= Before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 23 (804) |main_hall_style= [[Yuiitsu Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_names= |sacred_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }}
'''Yuki-no-Mikura''' (由貴御倉) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Kōtai Jingū). It is also referred to as '''Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami''' (由貴御倉神).<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref> It is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, standing north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿}}, alongside {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神}}.<ref name="ic"/>
== Overview == Yuki-no-Mikura is located in Ujikan-chō, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, within the precincts of the Inner Shrine. It is ranked 7th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine. The term "Yuki" (由貴) in its name means "pure and free from defilement."<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>
Yuki-no-Mikura was originally a storehouse for offering sacred provisions ({{ill|minie|ja|御贄}}) used in the {{ill|Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja|由貴大御饌}} ceremony.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The former shrine building served as a temporary main hall for {{ill|Yodo Shrine|ja|與杼神社}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, after the shrine was destroyed by fire, from 1975 (Shōwa 50) to 1979 (Shōwa 54).<ref>Yodo Shrine, "[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/syaden.html About the Shrine - Yodo Shrine, Guardian of Fushimi, Kyoto]" (Accessed October 13, 2013)</ref>
The shrine building is surrounded by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence). There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱}} (offering box).
The enshrined deity is Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami (由貴御倉神), who serves as the guardian deity of Yuki-no-Mikura.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus from the south exit of {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, or about 5 minutes south via {{ill|National Route 23|ja|国道23号}} from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami}}.<ref name="ic"/>
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Storehouses in Shinto shrines]] [[ja:由貴御倉]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4
== See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}}

{{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Yuki-no-Mikura |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yuki no Mikura 2.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ujikan-chō|ja|宇治館町}} 1, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, within the precincts of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 27 |latitude_sec = 19.5 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 43 |longitude_sec = 26.6 |map= Japan Mie |enshrined_deity= Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami |status= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine |established= Before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 23 (804) |main_hall_style= [[Yuiitsu Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_names= |sacred_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }}
'''Yuki-no-Mikura''' (由貴御倉) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Kōtai Jingū). It is also referred to as '''Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami''' (由貴御倉神).<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref> It is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, standing north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿}}, alongside {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神}}.<ref name="ic"/>
== Overview == Yuki-no-Mikura is located in Ujikan-chō, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, within the precincts of the Inner Shrine. It is ranked 7th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine. The term "Yuki" (由貴) in its name means "pure and free from defilement."<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>
Yuki-no-Mikura was originally a storehouse for offering sacred provisions ({{ill|minie|ja|御贄}}) used in the {{ill|Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja|由貴大御饌}} ceremony.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The former shrine building served as a temporary main hall for {{ill|Yodo Shrine|ja|與杼神社}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, after the shrine was destroyed by fire, from 1975 (Shōwa 50) to 1979 (Shōwa 54).<ref>Yodo Shrine, "[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/syaden.html About the Shrine - Yodo Shrine, Guardian of Fushimi, Kyoto]" (Accessed October 13, 2013)</ref>
The shrine building is surrounded by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence). There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱}} (offering box).
The enshrined deity is Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami (由貴御倉神), who serves as the guardian deity of Yuki-no-Mikura.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus from the south exit of {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, or about 5 minutes south via {{ill|National Route 23|ja|国道23号}} from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami}}.<ref name="ic"/>
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Storehouses in Shinto shrines]] [[ja:由貴御倉]]

{{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Yuki-no-Mikura |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yuki no Mikura 2.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ujikan-chō|ja|宇治館町}} 1, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, within the precincts of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 27 |latitude_sec = 19.5 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 43 |longitude_sec = 26.6 |map= Japan Mie |enshrined_deity= Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami |status= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine |established= Before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 23 (804) |main_hall_style= [[Yuiitsu Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_names= |sacred_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }}
'''Yuki-no-Mikura''' (由貴御倉) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Kōtai Jingū). It is also referred to as '''Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami''' (由貴御倉神).<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref> It is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, standing north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿}}, alongside {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神}}.<ref name="ic"/>
== Overview == Yuki-no-Mikura is located in Ujikan-chō, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, within the precincts of the Inner Shrine. It is ranked 7th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine. The term "Yuki" (由貴) in its name means "pure and free from defilement."<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>
Yuki-no-Mikura was originally a storehouse for offering sacred provisions ({{ill|minie|ja|御贄}}) used in the {{ill|Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja|由貴大御饌}} ceremony.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The former shrine building served as a temporary main hall for {{ill|Yodo Shrine|ja|與杼神社}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, after the shrine was destroyed by fire, from 1975 (Shōwa 50) to 1979 (Shōwa 54).<ref>Yodo Shrine, "[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/syaden.html About the Shrine - Yodo Shrine, Guardian of Fushimi, Kyoto]" (Accessed October 13, 2013)</ref>
The shrine building is surrounded by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence). There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱}} (offering box).
The enshrined deity is Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami (由貴御倉神), who serves as the guardian deity of Yuki-no-Mikura.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus from the south exit of {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, or about 5 minutes south via {{ill|National Route 23|ja|国道23号}} from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami}}.<ref name="ic"/>
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Storehouses in Shinto shrines]] [[ja:由貴御倉]]

{{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Yuki-no-Mikura |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yuki no Mikura 2.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ujikan-chō|ja|宇治館町}} 1, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, within the precincts of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 27 |latitude_sec = 19.5 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 43 |longitude_sec = 26.6 |map= Japan Mie |enshrined_deity= Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami |status= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine |established= Before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 23 (804) |main_hall_style= [[Yuiitsu Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_names= |sacred_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }}
'''Yuki-no-Mikura''' (由貴御倉) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Kōtai Jingū). It is also referred to as '''Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami''' (由貴御倉神).<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref> It is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, standing north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿}}, alongside {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神}}.<ref name="ic"/>
== Overview == Yuki-no-Mikura is located in Ujikan-chō, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, within the precincts of the Inner Shrine. It is ranked 7th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine. The term "Yuki" (由貴) in its name means "pure and free from defilement."<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>
Yuki-no-Mikura was originally a storehouse for offering sacred provisions ({{ill|minie|ja|御贄}}) used in the {{ill|Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja|由貴大御饌}} ceremony.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The former shrine building served as a temporary main hall for {{ill|Yodo Shrine|ja|與杼神社}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, after the shrine was destroyed by fire, from 1975 (Shōwa 50) to 1979 (Shōwa 54).<ref>Yodo Shrine, "[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/syaden.html About the Shrine - Yodo Shrine, Guardian of Fushimi, Kyoto]" (Accessed October 13, 2013)</ref>
The shrine building is surrounded by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence). There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱}} (offering box).
The enshrined deity is Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami (由貴御倉神), who serves as the guardian deity of Yuki-no-Mikura.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus from the south exit of {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, or about 5 minutes south via {{ill|National Route 23|ja|国道23号}} from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami}}.<ref name="ic"/>
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Storehouses in Shinto shrines]] [[ja:由貴御倉]]

{{125 Shrines of Ise|ゆきのみくら}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki-no-Mikura]] [[Category:由貴御倉]]<!--Yuki-no-Mikura-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki-no-Mikura]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuki-no-Mikura}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:由貴御倉]][[ja:由貴御倉]][[Category:由貴御倉]][[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:由貴御倉]][[Category:File]][[Category:File]] <!--Yuki-no-Mikura-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki-no-Mikura]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuki-no-Mikura}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Yuki-no-Mikura]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuki-no-Mikura]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki-no-Mikura]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki-no-Mikura]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki-no-Mikura]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuki-no-Mikura]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki-no-Mikura]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki-no-Mikura]][[Category:11]] [[en:Yuki-no-Mikura]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki-no-Mikura]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuki-no-Mikura]][[sn:uki-no-Mikura]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki-no-Mikura]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]]

{{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Yuki-no-Mikura |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yuki no Mikura 2.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ujikan-chō|ja|宇治館町}} 1, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, within the precincts of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 27 |latitude_sec = 19.5 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 43 |longitude_sec = 26.6 |map= Japan Mie |enshrined_deity= Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami |status= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine |established= Before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 23 (804) |main_hall_style= [[Yuiitsu Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_names= |sacred_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }}
'''Yuki-no-Mikura''' (由貴御倉) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Kōtai Jingū). It is also referred to as '''Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami''' (由貴御倉神).<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref> It is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, standing north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿}}, alongside {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神}}.<ref name="ic"/>
== Overview == Yuki-no-Mikura is located in Ujikan-chō, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, within the precincts of the Inner Shrine. It is ranked 7th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine. The term "Yuki" (由貴) in its name means "pure and free from defilement."<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>
Yuki-no-Mikura was originally a storehouse for offering sacred provisions ({{ill|minie|ja|御贄}}) used in the {{ill|Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja|由貴大御饌}} ceremony.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The former shrine building served as a temporary main hall for {{ill|Yodo Shrine|ja|與杼神社}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, after the shrine was destroyed by fire, from 1975 (Shōwa 50) to 1979 (Shōwa 54).<ref>Yodo Shrine, "[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/syaden.html About the Shrine - Yodo Shrine, Guardian of Fushimi, Kyoto]" (Accessed October 13, 2013)</ref>
The shrine building is surrounded by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence). There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱}} (offering box).
The enshrined deity is Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami (由貴御倉神), who serves as the guardian deity of Yuki-no-Mikura.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus from the south exit of {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, or about 5 minutes south via {{ill|National Route 23|ja|国道23号}} from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami}}.<ref name="ic"/>
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Storehouses in Shinto shrines]] [[ja:由貴御倉]]

== 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4

{{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Yuki-no-Mikura |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yuki no Mikura 2.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ujikan-chō|ja|宇治館町}} 1, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, within the precincts of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 27 |latitude_sec = 19.5 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 43 |longitude_sec = 26.6 |map= Japan Mie |enshrined_deity= Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami |status= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine |established= Before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 23 (804) |main_hall_style= [[Yuiitsu Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_names= |sacred_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }}
'''Yuki-no-Mikura''' (由貴御倉) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Kōtai Jingū). It is also referred to as '''Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami''' (由貴御倉神).<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref> It is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, standing north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿}}, alongside {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神}}.<ref name="ic"/>
== Overview == Yuki-no-Mikura is located in Ujikan-chō, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, within the precincts of the Inner Shrine. It is ranked 7th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine. The term "Yuki" (由貴) in its name means "pure and free from defilement."<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>
Yuki-no-Mikura was originally a storehouse for offering sacred provisions ({{ill|minie|ja|御贄}}) used in the {{ill|Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja|由貴大御饌}} ceremony.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The former shrine building served as a temporary main hall for {{ill|Yodo Shrine|ja|與杼神社}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, after the shrine was destroyed by fire, from 1975 (Shōwa 50) to 1979 (Shōwa 54).<ref>Yodo Shrine, "[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/syaden.html About the Shrine - Yodo Shrine, Guardian of Fushimi, Kyoto]" (Accessed October 13, 2013)</ref>
The shrine building is surrounded by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence). There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱}} (offering box).
The enshrined deity is Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami (由貴御倉神), who serves as the guardian deity of Yuki-no-Mikura.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus from the south exit of {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, or about 5 minutes south via {{ill|National Route 23|ja|国道23号}} from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami}}.<ref name="ic"/>
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Storehouses in Shinto shrines]] [[ja:由貴御倉]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵== See Also ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
{{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Yuki-no-Mikura |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yuki no Mikura 2.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ujikan-chō|ja|宇治館町}} 1, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, within the precincts of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 27 |latitude_sec = 19.5 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 43 |longitude_sec = 26.6 |map= Japan Mie |enshrined_deity= Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami |status= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine |established= Before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 23 (804) |main_hall_style= [[Yuiitsu Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_names= |sacred_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }}
'''Yuki-no-Mikura''' (由貴御倉) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Kōtai Jingū). It is also referred to as '''Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami''' (由貴御倉神).<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref> It is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, standing north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿}}, alongside {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神}}.<ref name="ic"/>
== Overview == Yuki-no-Mikura is located in Ujikan-chō, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, within the precincts of the Inner Shrine. It is ranked 7th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine. The term "Yuki" (由貴) in its name means "pure and free from defilement."<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>
Yuki-no-Mikura was originally a storehouse for offering sacred provisions ({{ill|minie|ja|御贄}}) used in the {{ill|Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja|由貴大御饌}} ceremony.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The former shrine building served as a temporary main hall for {{ill|Yodo Shrine|ja|與杼神社}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, after the shrine was destroyed by fire, from 1975 (Shōwa 50) to 1979 (Shōwa 54).<ref>Yodo Shrine, "[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/syaden.html About the Shrine - Yodo Shrine, Guardian of Fushimi, Kyoto]" (Accessed October 13, 2013)</ref>
The shrine building is surrounded by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence). There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱}} (offering box).
The enshrined deity is Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami (由貴御倉神), who serves as the guardian deity of Yuki-no-Mikura.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus from the south exit of {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, or about 5 minutes south via {{ill|National Route 23|ja|国道23号}} from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami}}.<ref name="ic"/>
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Storehouses in Shinto shrines]] [[ja:由貴御倉]]

== 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. revision.text:
{{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Yuki-no-Mikura |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yuki no Mikura 2.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ujikan-chō|ja|宇治館町}} 1, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, within the precincts of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 27 |latitude_sec = 19.5 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 43 |longitude_sec = 26.6 |map= Japan Mie |enshrined_deity= Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami |status= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine |established= Before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 23 (804) |main_hall_style= [[Yuiitsu Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_names= |sacred_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }}
'''Yuki-no-Mikura''' (由貴御倉) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Kōtai Jingū). It is also referred to as '''Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami''' (由貴御倉神).<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref> It is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, standing north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿}}, alongside {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神}}.<ref name="ic"/>
== Overview == Yuki-no-Mikura is located in Ujikan-chō, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, within the precincts of the Inner Shrine. It is ranked 7th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine. The term "Yuki" (由貴) in its name means "pure and free from defilement."<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>
Yuki-no-Mikura was originally a storehouse for offering sacred provisions ({{ill|minie|ja|御贄}}) used in the {{ill|Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja|由貴大御饌}} ceremony.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The former shrine building served as a temporary main hall for {{ill|Yodo Shrine|ja|與杼神社}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, after the shrine was destroyed by fire, from 1975 (Shōwa 50) to 1979 (Shōwa 54).<ref>Yodo Shrine, "[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/syaden.html About the Shrine - Yodo Shrine, Guardian of Fushimi, Kyoto]" (Accessed October 13, 2013)</ref>
The shrine building is surrounded by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence). There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱}} (offering box).
The enshrined deity is Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami (由貴御倉神), who serves as the guardian deity of Yuki-no-Mikura.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus from the south exit of {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, or about 5 minutes south via {{ill|National Route 23|ja|国道23号}} from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami}}.<ref name="ic"/>
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Storehouses in Shinto shrines]] [[ja:由貴御倉]]

{{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Yuki-no-Mikura |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yuki no Mikura 2.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ujikan-chō|ja|宇治館町}} 1, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, within the precincts of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 27 |latitude_sec = 19.5 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 43 |longitude_sec = 26.6 |map= Japan Mie |enshrined_deity= Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami |status= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine |established= Before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 23 (804) |main_hall_style= [[Yuiitsu Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_names= |sacred_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }}
'''Yuki-no-Mikura''' (由貴御倉) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Kōtai Jingū). It is also referred to as '''Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami''' (由貴御倉神).<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref> It is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, standing north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿}}, alongside {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神}}.<ref name="ic"/>
== Overview == Yuki-no-Mikura is located in Ujikan-chō, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, within the precincts of the Inner Shrine. It is ranked 7th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine. The term "Yuki" (由貴) in its name means "pure and free from defilement."<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>
Yuki-no-Mikura was originally a storehouse for offering sacred provisions ({{ill|minie|ja|御贄}}) used in the {{ill|Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja|由貴大御饌}} ceremony.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The former shrine building served as a temporary main hall for {{ill|Yodo Shrine|ja|與杼神社}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, after the shrine was destroyed by fire, from 1975 (Shōwa 50) to 1979 (Shōwa 54).<ref>Yodo Shrine, "[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/syaden.html About the Shrine - Yodo Shrine, Guardian of Fushimi, Kyoto]" (Accessed October 13, 2013)</ref>
The shrine building is surrounded by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence). There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱}} (offering box).
The enshrined deity is Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami (由貴御倉神), who serves as the guardian deity of Yuki-no-Mikura.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus from the south exit of {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, or about 5 minutes south via {{ill|National Route 23|ja|国道23号}} from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami}}.<ref name="ic"/>
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Storehouses in Shinto shrines]] [[ja:由貴御倉]]

{{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Yuki-no-Mikura |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yuki no Mikura 2.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ujikan-chō|ja|宇治館町}} 1, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, within the precincts of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 27 |latitude_sec = 19.5 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 43 |longitude_sec = 26.6 |map= Japan Mie |enshrined_deity= Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami |status= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine |established= Before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 23 (804) |main_hall_style= [[Yuiitsu Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_names= |sacred_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }}
'''Yuki-no-Mikura''' (由貴御倉) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Kōtai Jingū). It is also referred to as '''Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami''' (由貴御倉神).<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref> It is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, standing north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿}}, alongside {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神}}.<ref name="ic"/>
== Overview == Yuki-no-Mikura is located in Ujikan-chō, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, within the precincts of the Inner Shrine. It is ranked 7th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine. The term "Yuki" (由貴) in its name means "pure and free from defilement."<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>
Yuki-no-Mikura was originally a storehouse for offering sacred provisions ({{ill|minie|ja|御贄}}) used in the {{ill|Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja|由貴大御饌}} ceremony.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The former shrine building served as a temporary main hall for {{ill|Yodo Shrine|ja|與杼神社}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|ja|伏見区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, after the shrine was destroyed by fire, from 1975 (Shōwa 50) to 1979 (Shōwa 54).<ref>Yodo Shrine, "[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/syaden.html About the Shrine - Yodo Shrine, Guardian of Fushimi, Kyoto]" (Accessed October 13, 2013)</ref>
The shrine building is surrounded by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence). There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱}} (offering box).
The enshrined deity is Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami (由貴御倉神), who serves as the guardian deity of Yuki-no-Mikura.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus from the south exit of {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, or about 5 minutes south via {{ill|National Route 23|ja|国道23号}} from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami}}.<ref name="ic"/>
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Storehouses in Shinto shrines]] [[ja:由貴御倉]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 由貴御倉 |画像= [[ファイル:Yuki no Mikura 2.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]宇治館町1 内宮宮域内 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 19.5 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 26.6 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 由貴御倉神 |社格= [[皇大神宮]]所管社 |創建= [[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前 |本殿= [[唯一神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }}
'''由貴御倉'''（ゆきのみくら）は、[[伊勢神宮]][[皇大神宮]]（内宮）の所管社。'''由貴御倉神'''（ゆきのみくらのかみ）とも称する<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎（2008）：115ページ</ref><ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：16ページ</ref><ref name="gs2">櫻井（1970）：156ページ</ref>。内宮の宮域内、五丈殿の北側に[[御酒殿神]]と並び鎮座する<ref name="ic"/>。
== 概要 == [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]宇治館町、内宮の宮域に鎮座する。内宮の所管社30社のうち第7位である。社名の「由貴」とは、「清浄で[[穢れ]]のない」という意味である<ref name="gk">学研パブリッシング（2013）：56ページ</ref>。
由貴御倉は、由貴大御饌（ゆきのおおみけ）に供する御料の御贄（みにえ）を奉納する倉であった<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>。旧社殿は[[京都府]][[京都市]][[伏見区]]の[[與杼神社]]（よどじんじゃ）が[[火災]]で焼失した際に仮本殿として利用された（[[1975年]]〔昭和50年〕から[[1979年]]〔昭和54年〕まで）<ref>與杼神社"[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/syaden.html ご社殿へ - 與杼神社 〜伏見区淀の産土神〜]"（2013年10月13日閲覧。）</ref>。
社殿は[[玉垣]]に囲まれている。[[賽銭]]箱は置かれていない。
[[祭神]]は由貴御倉神（ゆきのみくらのかみ）<ref name="ic"/>。由貴御倉の[[守護神]]である<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>。
== 交通 == ; [[宇治橋 (伊勢市)|宇治橋]]前まで * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]]・[[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄山田線|山田線]][[伊勢市駅]]南口（JR側）または近鉄山田線・[[近鉄鳥羽線|鳥羽線]][[宇治山田駅]]より[[三重交通]]バスに15分ほど乗車、終点「内宮前」下車<ref name="mkr">三重県観光連盟"[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html 伊勢神宮（内宮）の観光施設・周辺情報 - 観光三重]"（2013年10月12日閲覧。）</ref>。近鉄鳥羽線[[五十鈴川駅]]からも三重交通バスで内宮前へ行くことが可能である。 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[伊勢西インターチェンジ|伊勢西IC]]より[[三重県道32号伊勢磯部線]]（御木本道路）を南へ約5分または[[伊勢インターチェンジ|伊勢IC]]より[[国道23号]]を南へ約5分、付近に[[駐車場]]あり<ref name="mkr"/>。 ; 宇治橋前から * 宇治橋から内宮（正宮）に向かって参道を進むと五丈殿がある<ref name="ic"/>。その北方に2つの所管社が並び、右側が由貴御倉、左側が御酒殿神である<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>。付近には[[四至神]]が鎮座する<ref name="ic"/>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4
== 関連項目 == * [[神宮125社の一覧]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0115.html 由貴御倉（ゆきのみくら）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会
{{神宮125社}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:ゆきのみくら}} [[Category:神宮125社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由貴御倉]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Yuki no Mikura.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.455417|N|136.724056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architecture_style={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17211882]] Wikidata name: Yuki-no-mikura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Yuki-no-Mikura |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yuki no Mikura 2.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ujikan-chō|ja|宇治館町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikan-chō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇治 (伊勢市)|1=Uji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449448}} 1, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], within the precincts of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 27 |latitude_sec = 19.5 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 43 |longitude_sec = 26.6 |map= Japan Mie |enshrined_deity= Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami |status= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine |established= Before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 23 (804) |main_hall_style= [[Yuiitsu Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_names= |sacred_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] }}
'''Yuki-no-Mikura''' (由貴御倉) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Kōtai Jingū). It is also referred to as '''Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami''' (由貴御倉神).<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref> It is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, standing north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, alongside {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/>
== Overview == Yuki-no-Mikura is located in Ujikan-chō, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], within the precincts of the Inner Shrine. It is Ranked 7th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine. The term "Yuki" (由貴) in its name means "pure and free from defilement."<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>
Yuki-no-Mikura was originally a storehouse for offering sacred provisions ({{ill|minie|ja|御贄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/minie|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) used in the {{ill|Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja|由貴大御饌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-no-Ōmike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ceremony.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The former shrine building served as a temporary main hall for {{ill|Yodo Shrine|ja|與杼神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodo Shrine|qq=|1=Yodo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613316}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], after the shrine was destroyed by fire, from 1975 (Shōwa 50) to 1979 (Shōwa 54).<ref>Yodo Shrine, "[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/syaden.html About the Shrine - Yodo Shrine, Guardian of Fushimi, Kyoto]" (Accessed October 13, 2013)</ref>
The shrine building is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence). There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|qq=|qid=Q135491444}} (offering box).
The enshrined deity is Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami (由貴御倉神), who serves as the guardian deity of Yuki-no-Mikura.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from the south exit of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] (JR side) on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]]/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|qq=|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], or about 5 minutes south via [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise IC|qq=|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yotsuishi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/> == References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0115.html 由貴御倉（ゆきのみくら）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会

== Overview == Yuki-no-Mikura is located in Ujikan-chō, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], within the precincts of the Inner Shrine. It is Ranked 7th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine. The term "Yuki" (由貴) in its name means "pure and free from defilement."<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>
Yuki-no-Mikura was originally a storehouse for offering sacred provisions ({{ill|minie|ja|御贄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/minie|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) used in the {{ill|Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja|由貴大御饌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-no-Ōmike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ceremony.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The former shrine building served as a temporary main hall for {{ill|Yodo Shrine|ja|與杼神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodo Shrine|qq=|1=Yodo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613316}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], after the shrine was destroyed by fire, from 1975 (Shōwa 50) to 1979 (Shōwa 54).<ref>Yodo Shrine, "[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/syaden.html About the Shrine - Yodo Shrine, Guardian of Fushimi, Kyoto]" (Accessed October 13, 2013)</ref>

Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|qq=|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], or about 5 minutes south via [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise IC|qq=|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yotsuishi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/> == References == {{Reflist}}

The shrine building is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence). There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444}} (offering box).
The enshrined deity is Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami (由貴御倉神), who serves as the guardian deity of Yuki-no-Mikura.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from the south exit of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] (JR side) on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]]/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yotsuishi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/> == References == {{Reflist}}

'''Yuki-no-Mikura''' (由貴御倉) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Kōtai Jingū). It is also referred to as '''Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami''' (由貴御倉神).<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref> It is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, standing north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, alongside {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/>
== Overview == Yuki-no-Mikura is located in Ujikan-chō, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], within the precincts of the Inner Shrine. It is Ranked 7th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine. The term "Yuki" (由貴) in its name means "pure and free from defilement."<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>
Yuki-no-Mikura was originally a storehouse for offering sacred provisions ({{ill|minie|ja|御贄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/minie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) used in the {{ill|Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja|由貴大御饌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ceremony.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The former shrine building served as a temporary main hall for {{ill|Yodo Shrine|ja|與杼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodo Shrine|1=Yodo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613316}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], after the shrine was destroyed by fire, from 1975 (Shōwa 50) to 1979 (Shōwa 54).<ref>Yodo Shrine, "[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/syaden.html About the Shrine - Yodo Shrine, Guardian of Fushimi, Kyoto]" (Accessed October 13, 2013)</ref>
The shrine building is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence). There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444}} (offering box).
The enshrined deity is Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami (由貴御倉神), who serves as the guardian deity of Yuki-no-Mikura.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由貴御倉]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Yuki no Mikura.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.455417|N|136.724056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architecture_style={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17211882]] Wikidata name: Yuki-no-mikura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Yuki-no-Mikura |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yuki no Mikura 2.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ujikan-chō|ja|宇治館町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikan-chō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇治 (伊勢市)|1=Uji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449448}} 1, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], within the precincts of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 27 |latitude_sec = 19.5 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 43 |longitude_sec = 26.6 |map= Japan Mie |enshrined_deity= Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami |status= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine |established= Before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 23 (804) |main_hall_style= [[Yuiitsu Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_names= |sacred_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] }}
'''Yuki-no-Mikura''' (由貴御倉) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Kōtai Jingū). It is also referred to as '''Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami''' (由貴御倉神).<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref> It is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, standing north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, alongside {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/>
== Overview == Yuki-no-Mikura is located in Ujikan-chō, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], within the precincts of the Inner Shrine. It is Ranked 7th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine. The term "Yuki" (由貴) in its name means "pure and free from defilement."<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>
Yuki-no-Mikura was originally a storehouse for offering sacred provisions ({{ill|minie|ja|御贄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/minie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) used in the {{ill|Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja|由貴大御饌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ceremony.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The former shrine building served as a temporary main hall for {{ill|Yodo Shrine|ja|與杼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodo Shrine|1=Yodo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613316}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], after the shrine was destroyed by fire, from 1975 (Shōwa 50) to 1979 (Shōwa 54).<ref>Yodo Shrine, "[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/syaden.html About the Shrine - Yodo Shrine, Guardian of Fushimi, Kyoto]" (Accessed October 13, 2013)</ref>
The shrine building is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence). There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444}} (offering box).
The enshrined deity is Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami (由貴御倉神), who serves as the guardian deity of Yuki-no-Mikura.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from the south exit of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] (JR side) on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]]/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], or about 5 minutes south via [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise IC|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yotsuishi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/> == References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0115.html 由貴御倉（ゆきのみくら）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会

== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from the south exit of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] (JR side) on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]]/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], or about 5 minutes south via [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise IC|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yotsuishi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/> == References == {{Reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由貴御倉]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Yuki no Mikura.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.455417|N|136.724056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architecture_style={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17211882]] Wikidata name: Yuki-no-mikura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Yuki-no-Mikura |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yuki no Mikura 2.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ujikan-chō|ja|宇治館町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikan-chō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇治 (伊勢市)|1=Uji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449448}} 1, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], within the precincts of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 27 |latitude_sec = 19.5 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 43 |longitude_sec = 26.6 |map= Japan Mie |enshrined_deity= Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami |status= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine |established= Before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 23 (804) |main_hall_style= [[Yuiitsu Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_names= |sacred_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] }}
'''Yuki-no-Mikura''' (由貴御倉) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Kōtai Jingū). It is also referred to as '''Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami''' (由貴御倉神).<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref> It is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, standing north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, alongside {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/>
== Overview == Yuki-no-Mikura is located in Ujikan-chō, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], within the precincts of the Inner Shrine. It is Ranked 7th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine. The term "Yuki" (由貴) in its name means "pure and free from defilement."<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>
Yuki-no-Mikura was originally a storehouse for offering sacred provisions ({{ill|minie|ja|御贄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/minie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) used in the {{ill|Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja|由貴大御饌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ceremony.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The former shrine building served as a temporary main hall for {{ill|Yodo Shrine|ja|與杼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodo Shrine|1=Yodo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613316}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], after the shrine was destroyed by fire, from 1975 (Shōwa 50) to 1979 (Shōwa 54).<ref>Yodo Shrine, "[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/syaden.html About the Shrine - Yodo Shrine, Guardian of Fushimi, Kyoto]" (Accessed October 13, 2013)</ref>
The shrine building is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence). There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444}} (offering box).
The enshrined deity is Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami (由貴御倉神), who serves as the guardian deity of Yuki-no-Mikura.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from the south exit of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] (JR side) on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]]/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], or about 5 minutes south via [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise IC|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yotsuishi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/> == References == {{Reflist}}
