<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由貴御倉]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Yuki no Mikura.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.455417|N|136.724056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architecture_style={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17211882]] Wikidata name: Yuki-no-mikura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Yuki-no-Mikura |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yuki no Mikura 2.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ujikan-chō|ja|宇治館町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikan-chō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇治 (伊勢市)|1=Uji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449448}} 1, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], within the precincts of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 27 |latitude_sec = 19.5 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 43 |longitude_sec = 26.6 |map= Japan Mie |enshrined_deity= Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami |status= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine |established= Before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 23 (804) |main_hall_style= [[Yuiitsu Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_names= |sacred_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] }}
'''Yuki-no-Mikura''' (由貴御倉) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Kōtai Jingū). It is also referred to as '''Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami''' (由貴御倉神).<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref> It is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, standing north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, alongside {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/>
== Overview == Yuki-no-Mikura is located in Ujikan-chō, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], within the precincts of the Inner Shrine. It is Ranked 7th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine. The term "Yuki" (由貴) in its name means "pure and free from defilement."<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>
Yuki-no-Mikura was originally a storehouse for offering sacred provisions ({{ill|minie|ja|御贄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/minie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) used in the {{ill|Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja|由貴大御饌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ceremony.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The former shrine building served as a temporary main hall for {{ill|Yodo Shrine|ja|與杼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodo Shrine|1=Yodo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613316}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], after the shrine was destroyed by fire, from 1975 (Shōwa 50) to 1979 (Shōwa 54).<ref>Yodo Shrine, "[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/syaden.html About the Shrine - Yodo Shrine, Guardian of Fushimi, Kyoto]" (Accessed October 13, 2013)</ref>
The shrine building is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence). There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444}} (offering box).
The enshrined deity is Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami (由貴御倉神), who serves as the guardian deity of Yuki-no-Mikura.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from the south exit of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] (JR side) on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]]/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], or about 5 minutes south via [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise IC|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yotsuishi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/> == References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}}

== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from the south exit of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] (JR side) on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]]/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], or about 5 minutes south via [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise IC|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yotsuishi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/> == References == {{Reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由貴御倉]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Yuki no Mikura.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.455417|N|136.724056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architecture_style={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17211882]] Wikidata name: Yuki-no-mikura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Yuki-no-Mikura |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yuki no Mikura 2.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ujikan-chō|ja|宇治館町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikan-chō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇治 (伊勢市)|1=Uji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449448}} 1, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], within the precincts of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 27 |latitude_sec = 19.5 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 43 |longitude_sec = 26.6 |map= Japan Mie |enshrined_deity= Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami |status= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine |established= Before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 23 (804) |main_hall_style= [[Yuiitsu Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_names= |sacred_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] }}
'''Yuki-no-Mikura''' (由貴御倉) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Kōtai Jingū). It is also referred to as '''Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami''' (由貴御倉神).<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref> It is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, standing north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, alongside {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/>
== Overview == Yuki-no-Mikura is located in Ujikan-chō, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], within the precincts of the Inner Shrine. It is Ranked 7th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine. The term "Yuki" (由貴) in its name means "pure and free from defilement."<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>
Yuki-no-Mikura was originally a storehouse for offering sacred provisions ({{ill|minie|ja|御贄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/minie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) used in the {{ill|Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja|由貴大御饌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ceremony.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The former shrine building served as a temporary main hall for {{ill|Yodo Shrine|ja|與杼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodo Shrine|1=Yodo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613316}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], after the shrine was destroyed by fire, from 1975 (Shōwa 50) to 1979 (Shōwa 54).<ref>Yodo Shrine, "[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/syaden.html About the Shrine - Yodo Shrine, Guardian of Fushimi, Kyoto]" (Accessed October 13, 2013)</ref>
The shrine building is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence). There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444}} (offering box).
The enshrined deity is Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami (由貴御倉神), who serves as the guardian deity of Yuki-no-Mikura.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from the south exit of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] (JR side) on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]]/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], or about 5 minutes south via [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise IC|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yotsuishi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/> == References == {{Reflist}}

ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由貴御倉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由貴御倉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由貴御倉]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Yuki no Mikura.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.455417|N|136.724056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architecture_style={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17211882]] Wikidata name: Yuki-no-mikura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Yuki-no-Mikura |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yuki no Mikura 2.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ujikan-chō|ja|宇治館町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikan-chō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇治 (伊勢市)|1=Uji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449448}} 1, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], within the precincts of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 27 |latitude_sec = 19.5 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 43 |longitude_sec = 26.6 |map= Japan Mie |enshrined_deity= Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami |status= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine |established= Before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 23 (804) |main_hall_style= [[Yuiitsu Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_names= |sacred_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] }}
'''Yuki-no-Mikura''' (由貴御倉) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Kōtai Jingū). It is also referred to as '''Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami''' (由貴御倉神).<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref> It is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, standing north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, alongside {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/>
== Overview == Yuki-no-Mikura is located in Ujikan-chō, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], within the precincts of the Inner Shrine. It is Ranked 7th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine. The term "Yuki" (由貴) in its name means "pure and free from defilement."<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>
Yuki-no-Mikura was originally a storehouse for offering sacred provisions ({{ill|minie|ja|御贄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/minie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) used in the {{ill|Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja|由貴大御饌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ceremony.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The former shrine building served as a temporary main hall for {{ill|Yodo Shrine|ja|與杼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodo Shrine|1=Yodo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613316}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], after the shrine was destroyed by fire, from 1975 (Shōwa 50) to 1979 (Shōwa 54).<ref>Yodo Shrine, "[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/syaden.html About the Shrine - Yodo Shrine, Guardian of Fushimi, Kyoto]" (Accessed October 13, 2013)</ref>
The shrine building is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence). There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444}} (offering box).
The enshrined deity is Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami (由貴御倉神), who serves as the guardian deity of Yuki-no-Mikura.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from the south exit of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] (JR side) on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]]/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], or about 5 minutes south via [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise IC|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yotsuishi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/> == References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}}

== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from the south exit of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] (JR side) on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]]/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], or about 5 minutes south via [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise IC|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yotsuishi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/> == References == {{Reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由貴御倉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由貴御倉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由貴御倉]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Yuki no Mikura.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.455417|N|136.724056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architecture_style={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17211882]] Wikidata name: Yuki-no-mikura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Yuki-no-Mikura |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yuki no Mikura 2.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ujikan-chō|ja|宇治館町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikan-chō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇治 (伊勢市)|1=Uji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449448}} 1, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], within the precincts of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 27 |latitude_sec = 19.5 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 43 |longitude_sec = 26.6 |map= Japan Mie |enshrined_deity= Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami |status= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine |established= Before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 23 (804) |main_hall_style= [[Yuiitsu Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_names= |sacred_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] }}
'''Yuki-no-Mikura''' (由貴御倉) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Kōtai Jingū). It is also referred to as '''Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami''' (由貴御倉神).<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref> It is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, standing north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, alongside {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/>
== Overview == Yuki-no-Mikura is located in Ujikan-chō, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], within the precincts of the Inner Shrine. It is Ranked 7th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine. The term "Yuki" (由貴) in its name means "pure and free from defilement."<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>
Yuki-no-Mikura was originally a storehouse for offering sacred provisions ({{ill|minie|ja|御贄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/minie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) used in the {{ill|Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja|由貴大御饌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ceremony.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The former shrine building served as a temporary main hall for {{ill|Yodo Shrine|ja|與杼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodo Shrine|1=Yodo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613316}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], after the shrine was destroyed by fire, from 1975 (Shōwa 50) to 1979 (Shōwa 54).<ref>Yodo Shrine, "[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/syaden.html About the Shrine - Yodo Shrine, Guardian of Fushimi, Kyoto]" (Accessed October 13, 2013)</ref>
The shrine building is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence). There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444}} (offering box).
The enshrined deity is Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami (由貴御倉神), who serves as the guardian deity of Yuki-no-Mikura.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from the south exit of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] (JR side) on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]]/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], or about 5 minutes south via [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise IC|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yotsuishi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/> == References == {{Reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由貴御倉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由貴御倉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由貴御倉]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Yuki no Mikura.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.455417|N|136.724056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architecture_style={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17211882]] Wikidata name: Yuki-no-mikura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Yuki-no-Mikura |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yuki no Mikura 2.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ujikan-chō|ja|宇治館町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikan-chō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇治 (伊勢市)|1=Uji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449448}} 1, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], within the precincts of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 27 |latitude_sec = 19.5 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 43 |longitude_sec = 26.6 |map= Japan Mie |enshrined_deity= Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami |status= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine |established= Before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 23 (804) |main_hall_style= [[Yuiitsu Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_names= |sacred_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] }}
'''Yuki-no-Mikura''' (由貴御倉) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Kōtai Jingū). It is also referred to as '''Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami''' (由貴御倉神).<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref> It is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, standing north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, alongside {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/>
== Overview == Yuki-no-Mikura is located in Ujikan-chō, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], within the precincts of the Inner Shrine. It is Ranked 7th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine. The term "Yuki" (由貴) in its name means "pure and free from defilement."<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>
Yuki-no-Mikura was originally a storehouse for offering sacred provisions ({{ill|minie|ja|御贄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/minie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) used in the {{ill|Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja|由貴大御饌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ceremony.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The former shrine building served as a temporary main hall for {{ill|Yodo Shrine|ja|與杼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodo Shrine|1=Yodo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613316}} in [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], after the shrine was destroyed by fire, from 1975 (Shōwa 50) to 1979 (Shōwa 54).<ref>Yodo Shrine, "[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/syaden.html About the Shrine - Yodo Shrine, Guardian of Fushimi, Kyoto]" (Accessed October 13, 2013)</ref>
The shrine building is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence). There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444}} (offering box).
The enshrined deity is Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami (由貴御倉神), who serves as the guardian deity of Yuki-no-Mikura.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from the south exit of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] (JR side) on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]]/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], or about 5 minutes south via [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise IC|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yotsuishi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/> == References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0115.html 由貴御倉（ゆきのみくら）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会

== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu}} bus from the south exit of {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise-shi Station}} (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=Sangū Line}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|en|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q900630|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line}}/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|en|Isuzugawa Station|WD=Q6089533|lt=Isuzugawa Station}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|en|Ise Expressway|WD=Q6079578|lt=Ise Expressway}}, or about 5 minutes south via {{ill|Japan National Route 23|en|Japan National Route 23|WD=Q5357579|lt=National Route 23}} from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise IC|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yotsuishi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/> == References == {{Reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由貴御倉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由貴御倉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由貴御倉]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Yuki no Mikura.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.455417|N|136.724056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architecture_style={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17211882]] Wikidata name: Yuki-no-mikura [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Yuki-no-Mikura |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yuki no Mikura 2.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ujikan-chō|ja|宇治館町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikan-chō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇治 (伊勢市)|1=Uji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449448}} 1, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, within the precincts of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 27 |latitude_sec = 19.5 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 43 |longitude_sec = 26.6 |map= Japan Mie |enshrined_deity= Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami |status= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine |established= Before {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku}} 23 (804) |main_hall_style= [[Yuiitsu Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_names= |sacred_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai}} }}
'''Yuki-no-Mikura''' (由貴御倉) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Kōtai Jingū). It is also referred to as '''Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami''' (由貴御倉神).<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref> It is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, standing north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, alongside {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/>
== Overview == Yuki-no-Mikura is located in Ujikan-chō, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie}}, within the precincts of the Inner Shrine. It is Ranked 7th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine. The term "Yuki" (由貴) in its name means "pure and free from defilement."<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>
Yuki-no-Mikura was originally a storehouse for offering sacred provisions ({{ill|minie|ja|御贄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/minie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) used in the {{ill|Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja|由貴大御饌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ceremony.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The former shrine building served as a temporary main hall for {{ill|Yodo Shrine|ja|與杼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodo Shrine|1=Yodo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613316}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|en|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1197771|lt=Fushimi-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}, after the shrine was destroyed by fire, from 1975 (Shōwa 50) to 1979 (Shōwa 54).<ref>Yodo Shrine, "[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/syaden.html About the Shrine - Yodo Shrine, Guardian of Fushimi, Kyoto]" (Accessed October 13, 2013)</ref>
The shrine building is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki}} (sacred fence). There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444}} (offering box).
The enshrined deity is Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami (由貴御倉神), who serves as the guardian deity of Yuki-no-Mikura.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu}} bus from the south exit of {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise-shi Station}} (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=Sangū Line}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|en|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q900630|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line}}/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|en|Isuzugawa Station|WD=Q6089533|lt=Isuzugawa Station}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|en|Ise Expressway|WD=Q6079578|lt=Ise Expressway}}, or about 5 minutes south via {{ill|Japan National Route 23|en|Japan National Route 23|WD=Q5357579|lt=National Route 23}} from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise IC|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yotsuishi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/> == References == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由貴御倉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由貴御倉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由貴御倉]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Yuki no Mikura.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.455417|N|136.724056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architecture_style={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}}}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Yuki-no-Mikura |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yuki no Mikura 2.jpg|270px]] |location= {{ill|Ujikan-chō|ja|宇治館町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikan-chō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇治 (伊勢市)|1=Uji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449448}} 1, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, within the precincts of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 27 |latitude_sec = 19.5 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 43 |longitude_sec = 26.6 |map= Japan Mie |enshrined_deity= Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami |status= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine |established= Before {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku}} 23 (804) |main_hall_style= [[Yuiitsu Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_names= |sacred_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai}} }}
'''Yuki-no-Mikura''' (由貴御倉) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Kōtai Jingū). It is also referred to as '''Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami''' (由貴御倉神).<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref> It is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, standing north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, alongside {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/>
== Overview == Yuki-no-Mikura is located in Ujikan-chō, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie}}, within the precincts of the Inner Shrine. It is Ranked 7th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine. The term "Yuki" (由貴) in its name means "pure and free from defilement."<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>
Yuki-no-Mikura was originally a storehouse for offering sacred provisions ({{ill|minie|ja|御贄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/minie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) used in the {{ill|Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja|由貴大御饌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ceremony.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The former shrine building served as a temporary main hall for {{ill|Yodo Shrine|ja|與杼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodo Shrine|1=Yodo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613316}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|en|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1197771|lt=Fushimi-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}, after the shrine was destroyed by fire, from 1975 (Shōwa 50) to 1979 (Shōwa 54).<ref>Yodo Shrine, "[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/syaden.html About the Shrine - Yodo Shrine, Guardian of Fushimi, Kyoto]" (Accessed October 13, 2013)</ref>
The shrine building is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki}} (sacred fence). There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444}} (offering box).
The enshrined deity is Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami (由貴御倉神), who serves as the guardian deity of Yuki-no-Mikura.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu}} bus from the south exit of {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise-shi Station}} (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=Sangū Line}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|en|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q900630|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line}}/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|en|Isuzugawa Station|WD=Q6089533|lt=Isuzugawa Station}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|en|Ise Expressway|WD=Q6079578|lt=Ise Expressway}}, or about 5 minutes south via {{ill|Japan National Route 23|en|Japan National Route 23|WD=Q5357579|lt=National Route 23}} from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise IC|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yotsuishi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/> == References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}}

'''Yuki-no-Mikura''' (由貴御倉) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Kōtai Jingū). It is also referred to as '''Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami''' (由貴御倉神).<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref> It is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, standing north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, alongside {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/>
== Overview == Yuki-no-Mikura is located in Ujikan-chō, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}, within the precincts of the Inner Shrine. It is Ranked 7th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine. The term "Yuki" (由貴) in its name means "pure and free from defilement."<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>
Yuki-no-Mikura was originally a storehouse for offering sacred provisions ({{ill|minie|ja|御贄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/minie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) used in the {{ill|Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja|由貴大御饌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ceremony.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The former shrine building served as a temporary main hall for {{ill|Yodo Shrine|ja|與杼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodo Shrine|1=Yodo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613316}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|en|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1197771|lt=Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|1=Fushimi-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}, after the shrine was destroyed by fire, from 1975 (Shōwa 50) to 1979 (Shōwa 54).<ref>Yodo Shrine, "[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/syaden.html About the Shrine - Yodo Shrine, Guardian of Fushimi, Kyoto]" (Accessed October 13, 2013)</ref>
The shrine building is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} (sacred fence). There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444|1=Saisenbako}} (offering box).
The enshrined deity is Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami (由貴御倉神), who serves as the guardian deity of Yuki-no-Mikura.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus from the south exit of {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise-shi Station|1=Iseshi Station}} (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=Sangū Line|1=Sangū Line}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|en|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q900630|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|1=Yamada Line (Kintetsu)}}/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|en|Isuzugawa Station|WD=Q6089533|lt=Isuzugawa Station|1=Isuzugawa Station}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|en|Ise Expressway|WD=Q6079578|lt=Ise Expressway|1=Ise Expressway}}, or about 5 minutes south via {{ill|Japan National Route 23|en|Japan National Route 23|WD=Q5357579|lt=National Route 23|1=Japan National Route 23}} from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise IC|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yotsuishi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/> == References == {{Reflist}}

ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}}

'''Yuki-no-Mikura''' (由貴御倉) is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Kōtai Jingū). It is also referred to as '''Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami''' (由貴御倉神).<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 115</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref> It is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, standing north of the {{ill|Gojōden|ja|五丈殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojōden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, alongside {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/>
== Overview == Yuki-no-Mikura is located in Ujikan-chō, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}, within the precincts of the Inner Shrine. It is Ranked 7th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine. The term "Yuki" (由貴) in its name means "pure and free from defilement."<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>
Yuki-no-Mikura was originally a storehouse for offering sacred provisions ({{ill|minie|ja|御贄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/minie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) used in the {{ill|Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja|由貴大御饌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ceremony.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The former shrine building served as a temporary main hall for {{ill|Yodo Shrine|ja|與杼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodo Shrine|1=Yodo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613316}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|en|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1197771|lt=Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|1=Fushimi-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}, after the shrine was destroyed by fire, from 1975 (Shōwa 50) to 1979 (Shōwa 54).<ref>Yodo Shrine, "[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/syaden.html About the Shrine - Yodo Shrine, Guardian of Fushimi, Kyoto]" (Accessed October 13, 2013)</ref>
The shrine building is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} (sacred fence). There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444|1=Saisenbako}} (offering box).
The enshrined deity is Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami (由貴御倉神), who serves as the guardian deity of Yuki-no-Mikura.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus from the south exit of {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise-shi Station|1=Iseshi Station}} (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=Sangū Line|1=Sangū Line}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|en|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q900630|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|1=Yamada Line (Kintetsu)}}/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|en|Isuzugawa Station|WD=Q6089533|lt=Isuzugawa Station|1=Isuzugawa Station}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|en|Ise Expressway|WD=Q6079578|lt=Ise Expressway|1=Ise Expressway}}, or about 5 minutes south via {{ill|Japan National Route 23|en|Japan National Route 23|WD=Q5357579|lt=National Route 23|1=Japan National Route 23}} from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise IC|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yotsuishi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/> == References == {{Reflist}}

It is Ranked 7th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine. The term "Yuki" (由貴) in its name means "pure and free from defilement."<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>
Yuki-no-Mikura was originally a storehouse for offering sacred provisions ({{ill|minie|ja|御贄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/minie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) used in the {{ill|Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja|由貴大御饌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuki-no-Ōmike|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ceremony.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The former shrine building served as a temporary main hall for {{ill|Yodo Shrine|ja|與杼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodo Shrine|1=Yodo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613316}} in {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|en|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1197771|lt=Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|1=Fushimi-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}, after the shrine was destroyed by fire, from 1975 (Shōwa 50) to 1979 (Shōwa 54).<ref>Yodo Shrine, "[http://www.yodojinja.com/yodojinja/syaden.html About the Shrine - Yodo Shrine, Guardian of Fushimi, Kyoto]" (Accessed October 13, 2013)</ref>
The shrine building is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} (sacred fence). There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444|1=Saisenbako}} (offering box).
The enshrined deity is Yuki-no-Mikura-no-Kami (由貴御倉神), who serves as the guardian deity of Yuki-no-Mikura.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus from the south exit of {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise-shi Station|1=Iseshi Station}} (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=Sangū Line|1=Sangū Line}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|en|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q900630|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|1=Yamada Line (Kintetsu)}}/{{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" bus stop (final stop).<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref> * Alternatively, from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|en|Isuzugawa Station|WD=Q6089533|lt=Isuzugawa Station|1=Isuzugawa Station}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line, take a Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Omikemichi) from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|en|Ise Expressway|WD=Q6079578|lt=Ise Expressway|1=Ise Expressway}}, or about 5 minutes south via {{ill|Japan National Route 23|en|Japan National Route 23|WD=Q5357579|lt=National Route 23|1=Japan National Route 23}} from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise IC|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}}. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
; From Uji Bridge * From Uji Bridge, proceed along the approach path towards the Inner Shrine (Main Shrine), and you will find the Gojōden.<ref name="ic"/> Two subordinate shrines are located to the north: Yuki-no-Mikura is on the right, and {{ill|Misakadono-no-Kami|ja|御酒殿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misakadono-no-Kami|qq=}} is on the left.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Nearby is the sacred site of {{ill|Yotsuishi-no-Kami|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yotsuishi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/> == References == {{Reflist}}
